name: Shift The Hot-Band / Peeled Seam Down One More 256-Row Chunk

summary.name: Shift The Hot-Band / Peeled Seam Down One More 256-Row Chunk
summary.hypothesis: Round 14/17 proved that the seam family is viable on top of the accepted PTX/export base: moving the pivot from 6400 to 6144 cut runtime from the failed broad control back down to 25.65470409 ms while keeping DRAM near 11.25 and long-scoreboard near 5.91. It still did not beat the accepted base, but it was the cleanest remaining direction. The next bounded step is to shift the pivot down one more 256-row chunk so the peeled path covers more of the bottom rows, without touching the PTX kernel internals or reopening any broad branch changes.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Boundary and launch split cost between the PTX hot-band kernel and the peeled 384-row row-band path.
summary.risk: Medium. The seam family improved cleanly once, but moving too much work into the peeled path can eventually reverse that trend.
summary.stop_condition: Stop the seam family if this second shift is slower than the 6144 pivot variant or pushes DRAM / barrier materially in the wrong direction.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
